era: Hip-Hop. E=7 -- third consecutive E=7 (181-183). Cold Recordings. Capitol Hill. Self-made infrastructure. E=7 trio closes. v1.49.324.

location.formative: Capitol Hill hip-hop scene. Independent infrastructure. Cold Recordings ecosystem.

members.name: Raz Simone
members.note: Capitol Hill. Cold Recordings founder. Self-made infrastructure.

associated_musicians.name: Macklemore
associated_musicians.relationship: Independent Seattle hip-hop. Degree 180. Macklemore LLC. Different era, same DIY tradition.

associated_musicians.name: Sir Mix-a-Lot
associated_musicians.relationship: Independent lineage. Degree 179. Nastymix Records. The original Seattle hip-hop infrastructure builder.

theory_nodes.concept: Self-made infrastructure -- Cold Recordings as label, brand, and platform. The DIY ethos of Seattle hip-hop carried to its logical extreme. Not just music -- entrepreneurial infrastructure.
examples: Cold Recordings: recording, distribution, visual branding.
Artist development platform for affiliated artists.

genealogy.relationship: Raz Simone at 183. Third generation of Seattle independent hip-hop infrastructure.

theory_nodes.concept: E=7 trio closes (181-183) -- three consecutive E=7 entries. Longest E=7 streak in the engine. Quadrant III opens at maximum energy.
theory_nodes.description: E=7 trio closes. Three consecutive maximum energy. Longest streak.

genealogy.relationship: Raz Simone at 183. Closes the E=7 trio.

theory_nodes.concept: Capitol Hill genre crossing -- hip-hop claiming space in indie/punk territory. Not displacement but cultural layering. Capitol Hill becomes more genre-diverse.
examples: Capitol Hill: indie rock, punk, LGBTQ+, arts.
Raz Simone brings hip-hop as primary genre.
Sub Pop territory meets Cold Recordings.
theory_nodes.description: Capitol Hill genre crossing. Hip-hop in indie territory.

genealogy.relationship: Raz Simone at 183. Hip-hop claims Capitol Hill space.

theory_nodes.concept: Independent label lineage -- Nastymix (179) -> Macklemore LLC (180-181) -> Cold Recordings (183). Three generations of Seattle hip-hop infrastructure. The tradition is entrepreneurial.
examples: Each generation builds its own infrastructure.
theory_nodes.description: Three generations of Seattle independent hip-hop infrastructure.

cross_references.relationship: E=7 STREAK! First of the trio. Macklemore LLC. Back-to-back #1 hits independently.
E=7 STREAK! Second of the trio. Nastymix. First gangsta rap.
LINEAGE! Nastymix -> Macklemore LLC -> Cold Recordings. Independent label lineage across three generations.
CAPITOL HILL! Both associated with Capitol Hill. Different eras, same neighborhood, same independence.
ENERGY DROP! After three consecutive E=7, Renz at E=6. The trio closes, energy descends.
